entries.reasoning: Correctly identifies both key points (modern 20,000-30,000 estimate vs. outdated 40,000 figure, and Andronikos Doukas desertion as primary cause) with appropriate historiographical context, though the added political analysis goes slightly beyond the stated ground truth.

All key facts match perfectly: Constantine IX initiated debasement in 1034 CE after 700+ years of stability at 4.48g/24 karats, falling to 8 karats under Nikephoros III (1078-1081), with correctly calculated 66% loss in purity; additional context strengthens the answer.
